evals.prompt: grill me on my plan to set up a centralized monitoring stack on Proxmox. I'm thinking I'll spin up a new LXC container and throw Grafana, Prometheus, and alerting on it. Also want it to watch the VMs and the network gear.
evals.expected_output: First question should be sharp and specific — targeting the most load-bearing unresolved decision (e.g. storage, alerting destination, data retention, HA). Should NOT ask multiple questions at once. Should NOT be generic. Should set up the interview framing briefly if needed.

assertions.name: single-question-only
assertions.description: Response contains at most 1 question mark (asks one focused question, not a list)

assertions.name: no-advice-before-questioning
assertions.description: Response does not lead with recommendations, suggestions, or how-to explanations before asking a question

assertions.name: no-affirmation-opener
assertions.description: Response does not open with hollow affirmations like 'Great plan!', 'That sounds solid', 'Nice approach'

assertions.name: specific-to-proxmox-or-stack
assertions.description: Question references something specific from the described plan (LXC, Grafana, Prometheus, network gear, VMs) rather than being a generic question

evals.prompt: I've been thinking about how to automate GIS staff onboarding. The plan is: when HR emails me a new hire, I parse the email, create their Google account, add them to the right groups, provision a Chromebook, and send them a welcome email. I want to build this as an n8n workflow.
evals.expected_output: Should pick the most dependency-heavy decision first (likely: how to reliably detect/parse the HR email, or what triggers the workflow). Should ask one focused question. Should demonstrate awareness of the implicit decisions embedded in the plan.

assertions.name: single-question-only
assertions.description: Response contains at most 1 question mark (asks one focused question, not a list)

assertions.name: no-advice-before-questioning
assertions.description: Response does not lead with recommendations, suggestions, or how-to explanations before asking a question

assertions.name: no-affirmation-opener
assertions.description: Response does not open with hollow affirmations like 'Great plan!', 'That sounds solid', 'Nice approach'

assertions.name: targets-trigger-or-parsing
assertions.description: First question targets how the HR email is detected/parsed or what triggers the workflow — the most fragile and critical dependency in the plan

evals.prompt: stress test my plan to move the GIS phone system from Spectrum to SpectrumVOIP. We're switching because it's cheaper and we can keep the same provider. The plan is basically: sign the contract, port the numbers, swap the hardware, done.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the biggest risk in the plan (likely: number porting timeline, service continuity, hardware compatibility, or rollback plan). First question should be concrete and specific to this plan — not generic VOIP migration questions.

assertions.name: single-question-only
assertions.description: Response contains at most 1 question mark (asks one focused question, not a list)

assertions.name: no-advice-before-questioning
assertions.description: Response does not lead with recommendations, suggestions, or how-to explanations before asking a question

assertions.name: no-affirmation-opener
assertions.description: Response does not open with hollow affirmations like 'Great plan!', 'That sounds solid', 'Nice approach'

assertions.name: targets-porting-or-continuity-or-hardware
assertions.description: First question targets number porting timeline, service continuity during cutover, or hardware compatibility — the most critical risks in a phone system migration
